Title: Add thumbnail task
Steps:
1. Navigate to Image Tools > Thumbnails
2. Click Add Task
3. Configure task name and settings
4. Save
Expected Result: New thumbnail task created

Title: Run thumbnail task
Steps:
1. Select thumbnail task
2. Click Run
3. Wait for completion
Expected Result: Thumbnail task executed; thumbnails generated

Title: Delete thumbnail task
Steps:
1. Select thumbnail task
2. Click Delete
3. Confirm deletion
Expected Result: Thumbnail task deleted; confirm dialog shown

Title: Save and reset task changes
Steps:
1. Make changes to task
2. Click Save
3. Make more changes
4. Click Reset
Expected Result: Save persists changes; Reset reverts to last saved state

Title: Thumbnail settings - background color
Steps:
1. Open thumbnail task
2. Navigate to Thumbnail Settings
3. Set Background Color
4. Save
Expected Result: Background color setting saved for thumbnails

Title: Thumbnail settings - anchor position
Steps:
1. Open thumbnail settings
2. Set Anchor Position
3. Save
Expected Result: Anchor position saved; thumbnails use configured position

Title: Thumbnail settings - JPEG quality (low and very high)
Steps:
1. Set JPEG quality to Low; generate thumbnail
2. Set JPEG quality to Very High; generate thumbnail
3. Compare file sizes
Expected Result: Low quality produces smaller files; Very High produces larger/sharper files

Title: Thumbnail resize method - FixedSize
Steps:
1. Set resize method to FixedSize
2. Set width and height
3. Run task and verify output
Expected Result: Thumbnails generated at exact specified dimensions

Title: Thumbnail resize method - FixedWidth and FixedHeight
Steps:
1. Set resize method to FixedWidth; run task
2. Set resize method to FixedHeight; run task
3. Verify dimensions
Expected Result: Width-fixed maintains aspect ratio; Height-fixed maintains aspect ratio

Title: Thumbnail resize method - Crop
Steps:
1. Set resize method to Crop
2. Set dimensions
3. Run task and verify
Expected Result: Thumbnail cropped to exact dimensions

Title: Add and manage task settings
Steps:
1. Open task
2. Add settings (paths and configurations)
3. Save changes
4. Reset changes to verify
Expected Result: Settings added; save persists; reset reverts to previous state

Title: Enter path manually and select multiple paths
Steps:
1. Enter image path manually
2. Select several paths from list
3. Verify all paths included
Expected Result: Manual and list-selected paths all included in task

Title: Manage multiple settings in task
Steps:
1. Add several settings entries
2. Remove settings from list
3. Save
Expected Result: Multiple settings managed correctly; removal works

Title: Process changes vs Regenerate thumbnails
Steps:
1. Run task with Process Changes option
2. Run task with Regenerate option
3. Compare behavior
Expected Result: Process Changes updates only changed images; Regenerate rebuilds all

Title: Process several tasks
Steps:
1. Select multiple tasks
2. Process all
Expected Result: All selected tasks processed successfully

Title: Image Tools permissions - CRUD
Steps:
1. Test Access permission
2. Test Read permission
3. Test Create permission
4. Test Update permission
5. Test Delete permission
Expected Result: Each permission level grants appropriate access

Title: Enable image processing job
Steps:
1. Navigate to Settings > Image Tools
2. Toggle Enable Image Processing Job ON
3. Set cron expression
4. Set partition size
5. Save
Expected Result: Image processing job scheduled with configured settings

Title: Disable image processing job
Steps:
1. Toggle Enable Image Processing Job OFF
2. Save
Expected Result: Image processing job disabled; no automatic processing

Title: Refresh tasks list and UI operations
Steps:
1. Click Refresh on tasks list
2. Add/remove columns
3. Change column size
4. Toggle full screen
Expected Result: UI operations work correctly; list refreshes

Title: Thumbnail background work setup
Steps:
1. Navigate to Thumbnails
2. Set up background work configuration
3. Verify RUN general view
Expected Result: Background work configured; run view shows task status

Title: Thumbnail Size Configuration — Small
Preconditions: Thumbnail task exists; admin logged into ${BACK_URL}
Steps:
1. Navigate to ${BACK_URL} > Image Tools > Thumbnails
2. Open an existing thumbnail task or create a new one
3. Locate the Width and Height dimension fields
4. Enter 100 in the Width field
5. Enter 100 in the Height field
6. Click Save
7. Run the thumbnail task on a sample product image
8. Navigate to the generated thumbnail output path and verify the file
Expected Result:
- Width field accepts value 100
- Height field accepts value 100
- Settings saved without validation errors
- Thumbnail task runs successfully
- Generated thumbnail file dimensions are exactly 100x100 pixels
- Thumbnail file size is smaller than the original image

Title: Thumbnail Size Configuration — Medium
Preconditions: Thumbnail task exists; admin logged into ${BACK_URL}
Steps:
1. Navigate to ${BACK_URL} > Image Tools > Thumbnails
2. Open an existing thumbnail task
3. Set Width field to 300
4. Set Height field to 300
5. Click Save
6. Run the thumbnail task on a sample product image
7. Verify the generated thumbnail dimensions
8. Compare file size to the 100x100 output from small configuration
Expected Result:
- Width and Height fields both accept value 300
- Settings saved successfully
- Generated thumbnail dimensions are exactly 300x300 pixels
- File size is larger than 100x100 thumbnail but smaller than original
- Task completes without errors

Title: Thumbnail Size Configuration — Large
Preconditions: Thumbnail task exists; admin logged into ${BACK_URL}
Steps:
1. Navigate to ${BACK_URL} > Image Tools > Thumbnails
2. Open an existing thumbnail task
3. Set Width field to 800
4. Set Height field to 800
5. Click Save
6. Run the thumbnail task on a sample product image
7. Inspect the generated thumbnail file dimensions
8. Verify that file size is proportionally larger than medium (300x300) output
Expected Result:
- Width and Height fields both accept value 800
- Settings saved successfully
- Generated thumbnail dimensions are exactly 800x800 pixels
- File size is larger than both 100x100 and 300x300 thumbnails
- Quality is visibly higher at this resolution
- Task completes without errors

Title: Thumbnail Quality Setting — JPEG 50% Compression
Preconditions: Thumbnail task with JPEG output configured; admin logged into ${BACK_URL}
Steps:
1. Navigate to ${BACK_URL} > Image Tools > Thumbnails
2. Open a thumbnail task configured for JPEG output
3. Locate the JPEG Quality setting field
4. Set JPEG quality to 50 (or equivalent Mid/Low setting)
5. Click Save
6. Run the thumbnail task on a known reference image
7. Record the output file size
8. Set JPEG quality to 95 (High) and repeat the task on the same image
9. Compare the two output file sizes and visual quality
Expected Result:
- Quality field accepts value 50 without validation error
- Settings saved successfully at 50% quality
- Output file at 50% quality has measurably smaller file size than 95% quality output
- Visual quality at 50% is acceptable but shows compression artifacts compared to 95%
- Both tasks complete without errors
- File size difference is at least 30% smaller at 50% vs 95%

Title: Batch Image Processing — Multiple Products
Preconditions: Multiple products with images exist; thumbnail task configured; admin logged into ${BACK_URL}
Steps:
1. Navigate to ${BACK_URL} > Image Tools > Thumbnails
2. Open a thumbnail task configured with a path covering multiple product images
3. Ensure at least 5 product images are present in the configured path
4. Click Run on the thumbnail task
5. Observe the processing execution
6. Wait for the task to complete
7. Navigate to the output path and count generated thumbnail files
8. Verify a sample of 3 thumbnails for correct dimensions
Expected Result:
- Task starts processing without errors
- All product images in the configured path are included in processing
- Generated thumbnail count matches the number of source images
- Each inspected thumbnail has the configured dimensions
- No errors reported for individual image processing failures
- Task status shows 'Completed' after all images are processed

Title: Thumbnail Task Progress Monitoring
Preconditions: Thumbnail task configured with multiple product images; admin logged into ${BACK_URL}
Steps:
1. Navigate to ${BACK_URL} > Image Tools > Thumbnails
2. Select a thumbnail task that will process a large number of images (10+)
3. Click Run to start the task
4. Immediately observe the task list or task detail view for progress indicators
5. Note the progress percentage or processed count while task is running
6. Monitor for any intermediate status updates during processing
7. Observe when processing reaches 100% or completes
8. Verify the final completed status display
Expected Result:
- Progress indicator is visible while the batch task is running
- Progress value increases over time (e.g., percentage or items processed count)
- UI does not freeze or become unresponsive during processing
- Task status transitions from 'Running' to 'Completed' after all images are processed
- Final status clearly indicates completion
- No timeout or error during the monitoring period

Title: Tooltip Validation on Thumbnail Settings
Preconditions: Thumbnail task settings panel open; admin logged into ${BACK_URL}
Steps:
1. Navigate to ${BACK_URL} > Image Tools > Thumbnails
2. Open a thumbnail task to access its settings panel
3. Hover the mouse cursor over the Width field label or its info icon
4. Wait 1-2 seconds for tooltip to appear and record the tooltip text
5. Hover over the Height field label or its info icon
6. Record the tooltip text
7. Hover over the JPEG Quality field label or its info icon
8. Hover over the Resize Method dropdown label or its info icon
Expected Result:
- Tooltip appears on hover for each settings field within 2 seconds
- Tooltip text is descriptive and accurately explains the field's purpose
- Tooltips disappear when the cursor moves away
- No tooltip shows raw key names, HTML tags, or empty content
- All tooltips are readable and correctly localized in the active UI language

Title: Thumbnail Output Format — PNG vs JPEG
Preconditions: Thumbnail task with configurable output format; product images available; admin logged into ${BACK_URL}
Steps:
1. Navigate to ${BACK_URL} > Image Tools > Thumbnails
2. Open a thumbnail task and locate the Output Format setting
3. Set Output Format to JPEG
4. Set dimensions to 300x300 and quality to 85
5. Run the task on a sample product image
6. Download and inspect the output file extension and format
7. Change Output Format to PNG
8. Run the task on the same sample product image
9. Download and inspect the PNG output file
Expected Result:
- JPEG output format produces a file with .jpg or .jpeg extension
- JPEG file uses lossy compression; file size is smaller than PNG for the same image
- PNG output format produces a file with .png extension
- PNG file supports lossless compression; file size is typically larger than JPEG
- Both formats render the image at the correct 300x300 dimensions
- PNG output preserves transparency if the source image has an alpha channel
- Both tasks complete without errors
